skill.name: hypogenic
skill.description: Guide automated hypothesis generation, refinement, and testing workflows with the Hypogenic research framework.
skill.summary: Generate and test research hypotheses from datasets, papers, and LLM-assisted analysis workflows.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported a high volume of command, network, API key, weak-crypto, and C2 keyword matches. Review found no malicious intent, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, crypto implementation, or C2 behavior; most blocker matches are false positives in documentation and configuration text. The skill remains medium risk because it instructs users to install packages, clone external repositories, run local scripts, and configure LLM API keys for research data workflows.

medium_findings.title: User-Directed Shell Commands and Script Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill documents commands that install packages, clone datasets, start services, and run local scripts. This is expected for a research tooling skill, but users should review packages, repositories, and scripts before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible examples in documentation, not hidden execution paths. The risk is moderate because following them can execute third-party or local code.

medium_findings.title: LLM API Workflow May Process Sensitive Research Data
medium_findings.description: The configuration template names an API key environment variable and the skill describes API-based LLM workflows over datasets and literature. No credential exfiltration was found, but users should avoid sending restricted data to external models without approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly supports API-based LLM use and configures an API key environment variable. There is no evidence of hidden network exfiltration or embedded secrets.

low_findings.title: External URLs Used for Documentation and Datasets
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to GitHub repositories, arXiv papers, ACL Anthology, PyPI, and project support pages. These are expected references, but users should verify external resources before downloading or running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible citations, repository links, package links, and dataset clone targets. They do not show covert outbound requests.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Keyword Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography, C2 keyword, and reconnaissance matches occur in method names, publication descriptions, package names, labels, or troubleshooting text. No cryptographic implementation, command-and-control behavior, or system reconnaissance logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review confirms these matches are documentation or YAML example text. I found no code implementing weak algorithms, C2 logic, or host reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Research teams often need many testable hypotheses before they can compare evidence. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code guide Hypogenic workflows for dataset-driven and literature-informed discovery.
actual_capabilities: Explains HypoGeniC, HypoRefine, and Union hypothesis generation methods.
Provides CLI and Python workflow guidance for generating and testing hypotheses.
Describes dataset format requirements for train, validation, and test data.
Guides literature preprocessing with GROBID for paper-informed hypotheses.
Shows how to configure prompt templates, labels, caching, and output settings.
Offers troubleshooting advice for generic hypotheses, inference quality, and label extraction.
limitations: Does not include the Hypogenic package source code or datasets inside the skill.
Requires users to install external tools and verify downloaded repositories.
May require LLM API credentials or approved local model infrastructure.
Generated hypotheses still need human review and empirical validation.

use_cases.title: Explore New Dataset Patterns
use_cases.description: Generate candidate hypotheses from labeled observational data when prior theory is limited.

use_cases.title: Combine Papers with Evidence
use_cases.description: Use literature-informed workflows to compare theory-based ideas with patterns found in data.

use_cases.title: Benchmark Hypothesis Methods
use_cases.description: Compare HypoGeniC, HypoRefine, Union methods, and baselines on a controlled research task.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Hypothesis Project
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new dataset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a Hypogenic project for a binary classification dataset. Explain the required files, labels, and configuration fields.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Data-Driven Hypotheses
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the HypoGeniC workflow to plan hypothesis generation from my train and validation data. Include the CLI steps and review checks.

prompt_templates.title: Add Literature Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a HypoRefine workflow for my research question. Include paper preprocessing, prompt templates, and how to compare literature and data hypotheses.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate and Refine a Study
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my Hypogenic experiment plan for validity, data leakage, label extraction errors, and hypothesis redundancy. Suggest a safer evaluation design.

output_examples.input: I have labeled reviews and want hypotheses about deceptive language.
output_examples.output: The skill would outline dataset formatting, propose a HypoGeniC run, define label extraction checks, and suggest reviewing hypotheses for measurable linguistic cues.

output_examples.input: I want to combine ten papers with social media stress data.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend a HypoRefine workflow, paper preprocessing steps, prompt template areas, and a comparison between literature-only and data-driven hypotheses.

output_examples.input: My inference results are poor after hypothesis generation.
output_examples.output: The skill would check label formatting, training coverage, generic hypotheses, prompt specificity, and whether more targeted refinement examples are needed.

best_practices: Review external repositories and scripts before running installation or preprocessing commands.
Keep restricted datasets out of external LLM APIs unless approval and data controls are in place.
Validate every generated hypothesis with held-out data and human domain review.
anti_patterns: Treating generated hypotheses as scientific conclusions without empirical testing.
Sending confidential research data to API models without governance review.
Changing label extraction rules after seeing test results.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan and run Hypogenic workflows for automated hypothesis generation, refinement, and testing.

faq.question: Does it include the Hypogenic software?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and references for installing and using the external Hypogenic package.

faq.question: Can it work without research papers?
faq.answer: Yes. The HypoGeniC method can generate hypotheses from observational data without literature input.

faq.question: When should I use HypoRefine?
faq.answer: Use HypoRefine when relevant papers can provide theory or prior evidence for the research task.

faq.question: Are API keys required?
faq.answer: They may be required for API-based LLM models. Local model workflows depend on your own infrastructure.

faq.question: Is this safe for private data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Review model providers, logs, scripts, and data policies before processing sensitive datasets.
